edges.description: Persistent DNA repair failure contributes to impaired somatic and brain growth.

edges.description: Failed lymphocyte receptor rearrangement compromises adaptive immune development.

edges.description: Impaired repair of DNA double-strand breaks causes increased sensitivity to ionizing radiation.
edges.source: Defective V(D)J recombination and lymphocyte development

edges.description: Systemic developmental impairment contributes to severe growth retardation.

nodes.description: Combined immunodeficiency is a central clinical manifestation of Cernunnos-XLF deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of NHEJ1 Protein Due to a Novel Splice Site Mutation in a Family Presenting with Combined Immunodeficiency, Microcephaly, and Growth Retardation and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: PURPOSE: We are reporting the first family from the Arabian Gulf with three siblings presenting with combined immunodeficiency (CID), microcephaly, and growth retardation due to a novel NHEJ1 splice site mutation, in addition to a review of the previously published literature on this subject.

nodes.description: NHEJ pathway failure impairs repair of programmed DNA breaks during B-cell and T-cell development, producing combined immunodeficiency.
nodes.id: Defective V(D)J recombination and lymphocyte development

evidence.reference_title: Programmed DNA breaks in lymphoid cells: repair mechanisms and consequences in human disease.
evidence.snippet: Here we review the mechanisms that repair programmed DNA lesions that occur during B-cell and T-cell development, as well as human diseases that arise through defects in these pathways.

nodes.description: NHEJ1 deficiency causes a syndromic developmental phenotype with impaired growth and reduced brain growth in addition to immunodeficiency.
nodes.id: Developmental growth impairment from DNA repair failure

evidence.reference_title: Loss of NHEJ1 Protein Due to a Novel Splice Site Mutation in a Family Presenting with Combined Immunodeficiency, Microcephaly, and Growth Retardation and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: PURPOSE: We are reporting the first family from the Arabian Gulf with three siblings presenting with combined immunodeficiency (CID), microcephaly, and growth retardation due to a novel NHEJ1 splice site mutation, in addition to a review of the previously published literature on this subject.

nodes.description: Severe growth retardation is part of the syndromic phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of NHEJ1 Protein Due to a Novel Splice Site Mutation in a Family Presenting with Combined Immunodeficiency, Microcephaly, and Growth Retardation and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: PURPOSE: We are reporting the first family from the Arabian Gulf with three siblings presenting with combined immunodeficiency (CID), microcephaly, and growth retardation due to a novel NHEJ1 splice site mutation, in addition to a review of the previously published literature on this subject.

nodes.description: Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation can provide curative treatment for the severe immunodeficiency component of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Two SCID cases with Cernunnos-XLF deficiency successfully treated by hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
evidence.snippet: These patients received hematopoietic stem cells from HLA identical related donor without conditioning regimen and recovered without any complication.

nodes.description: Regular IVIG prophylaxis can reduce infection burden in affected individuals with antibody deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Cernunnos/XLF Deficiency: A Syndromic Primary Immunodeficiency.
evidence.snippet: She is now on regular IVIG prophylaxis and has no new infection.

nodes.description: Microcephaly is a recurring syndromic feature in Cernunnos-XLF deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Cernunnos/XLF Deficiency: A Syndromic Primary Immunodeficiency.
evidence.snippet: In conclusion, the patients with microcephaly, bird-like face, and severe growth retardation should be evaluated for hypogammaglobulinemia and primary immunodeficiency diseases.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of NHEJ1 Protein Due to a Novel Splice Site Mutation in a Family Presenting with Combined Immunodeficiency, Microcephaly, and Growth Retardation and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: Patients were found to have a homozygous splice site mutation immediately downstream of exon 3 in NHEJ1 (c.390 + 1G > C).
NHEJ1 | HGNC:25737 | Cernunnos-XLF deficiency | MONDO:0012650 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Cernunnos-XLF deficiency results from NHEJ1 dysfunction within the nonhomologous end joining DNA repair pathway, producing a radiosensitive DNA repair defect.

biological_processes: double-strand break repair via nonhomologous end joining

evidence.reference_title: Cernunnos/XLF Deficiency: A Syndromic Primary Immunodeficiency.
evidence.snippet: Artemis, DNA ligase IV, DNA protein kinase catalytic subunit, and Cernunnos/XLF genes in nonhomologous end joining pathways of DNA repair mechanisms have been identified as responsible for radiosensitive SCID.

nodes.description: Radiosensitivity is a cardinal feature of Cernunnos-XLF deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Cernunnos/XLF Deficiency: A Syndromic Primary Immunodeficiency.
evidence.snippet: Artemis, DNA ligase IV, DNA protein kinase catalytic subunit, and Cernunnos/XLF genes in nonhomologous end joining pathways of DNA repair mechanisms have been identified as responsible for radiosensitive SCID.
